summary: FF PAYALUGA is a private Free Fire esports tournament web application where users register with auto-generated IDs, deposit funds (₹10-₹100) via UPI ID (NIRANJAN0508@FAM), join 1v1 Clash Squad tournaments using wallet coins, and submit their FF UID + WhatsApp number. Features a comprehensive admin panel for deposit verification, coin management, tournament control, and player detail viewing. Dark Free Fire gaming theme with mobile-responsive design.
architectural_notes: Private access web application with manual link sharing
Manual admin verification workflow for deposits and coin additions
Manual room creation and WhatsApp-based room detail distribution by admin
Auto-generated unique user IDs upon registration
Real-time tournament join requests sent to admin dashboard
UPI ID-based payment flow (NIRANJAN0508@FAM) instead of QR code scanning
Admin panel secured with password authentication (admin123)
Real-time notification system for deposit requests in admin dashboard
Two separate password-protected admin websites: one for tournament match management, one for coin deposits
known_issues: 'ACTOR NOT AVAILABLE' error occurring during deposit submission - requires backend actor initialization fix
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Free Fire battle background with combat zone environment and battlefield elements (ff-battle-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
- Free Fire themed header background with game UI elements (ff-header-bg.dim_1200x400.png)

existing_features.title: Implement user authentication system with email and password registration. Upon successful registration, automatically generate and assign a unique User ID to each user. Store user credentials, email, and generated ID in the backend.

Create a wallet system for each user with coin balance tracking. Implement deposit functionality where users can enter amounts between ₹10 and ₹100. Display a Google Pay QR code when user submits a deposit request. Store pending deposit requests with amount and timestamp.

Build admin deposit verification system. Admin can view all pending deposit requests with user details and submitted amounts. Admin can approve deposits and manually add equivalent coins to user wallets using 1₹ = 1 Coin conversion rate.

Create comprehensive admin control panel with user management, tournament configuration, and match scheduling. Admin can view all registered users with their IDs, manage tournament status (Open/Closed), set tournament entry fee, set match time, and customize match schedule. Admin can view all tournament join requests with player UID and WhatsApp numbers.

Design and implement Free Fire gaming themed UI with dark background throughout the application. Use gaming-style elements including animated buttons, esports-inspired layouts, and Free Fire visual motifs. Ensure all pages are fully mobile responsive with touch-friendly controls and adaptive layouts.

Create six-page application structure: Home page (landing/welcome), Login/Register page (authentication forms), Dashboard (user overview with wallet balance and tournament status), Wallet/Deposit page (deposit form and transaction history), Tournament Page (join form and active tournaments), and Admin Panel (user management, deposit verification, tournament control).

Replace the existing Google Pay QR code displayed on the Wallet/Deposit page with the user-provided payment QR code image (Screenshot_20260219_235411.jpg). Display this QR code when a user successfully submits a deposit request between ₹10-₹100.
Add a visible website URL link or shareable website address to the application interface. The link should allow users to easily copy or share the FF PAYALUGA platform URL.
Remove the Google Pay QR code image display from the Wallet/Deposit page (WalletDeposit.tsx) after a user submits a deposit request

Display the UPI ID 'NIRANJAN0508@FAM' on the Wallet/Deposit page after a user successfully submits a deposit request between ₹10-₹100. Show clear instructions for users to complete payment using this UPI ID.

Investigate and fix the 'ACTOR NOT AVAILABLE' error occurring when users submit deposit requests on the Wallet/Deposit page. Ensure the backend actor is properly initialized and accessible during deposit submission.

Implement password-based authentication for the admin panel. Only users who enter the correct password 'admin123' can access admin controls. Add a password input screen before showing the admin dashboard.
Display tournament join request details (user UID and WhatsApp number) in the admin panel when users join tournaments. Ensure this information is clearly visible and accessible for the admin.

Add a real-time notification system in the admin panel that alerts the admin whenever a user submits a deposit request. Display notification badges or alerts showing the count of pending deposit requests.

Add tournament match management interface to the admin panel where admin can directly conduct tournament matches. Include functionality to view current matches, update match status, record results, and manage match schedules.

Add direct coin deposit functionality in the admin panel where admin can manually add coins to any user's wallet without requiring a deposit request. Include user selection interface and coin amount input with validation.

Replace current background styling across all pages with authentic Free Fire battle-themed backgrounds featuring battlefield environments, combat zones, and in-game visual elements characteristic of real Free Fire gameplay.

Update the application's color scheme and theme styling to match authentic Free Fire battle aesthetics, including Free Fire's signature color palette, UI patterns from the actual game, and combat-inspired visual elements throughout all components and pages.
Display the owner name 'NIRANJAN' prominently in the application interface, ensuring it is clearly visible and appropriately styled to match the Free Fire battle theme.

new_feature_requests.summary: Authentication system with email/password login and auto-generated unique user IDs
Wallet system with ₹10-₹100 deposit limits, Google Pay QR code display, and coin management (1₹ = 1 Coin)
Tournament join system for 1v1 Clash Squad with FF UID and WhatsApp submission, automatic wallet deduction
Admin panel for user management, deposit verification, coin addition, tournament control, and player detail viewing
Free Fire gaming theme with dark UI, animated buttons, and mobile-responsive design
Six-page structure: Home, Login/Register, Dashboard, Wallet/Deposit, Tournament Page, Admin Panel

Implement tournament join system for Free Fire Clash Squad 1v1 mode. Users must provide their Free Fire UID and WhatsApp phone number when joining. Automatically deduct the tournament entry fee from user's wallet balance. Send user UID, phone number, and join timestamp to admin dashboard instantly.
Replace QR code payment with UPI ID display (NIRANJAN0508@FAM) for deposit transactions
Fix 'ACTOR NOT AVAILABLE' error occurring during deposit submission
Implement admin panel password protection with secret password 'admin123' - only authorized users can access admin controls
Add real-time notification system in admin panel to alert when users make deposit requests
Create backend functions to support admin tournament match management including storing match data, updating match status, and recording match results with participant information.
Create backend function for admin direct coin deposits that adds coins to user wallets without requiring user-initiated deposit requests. Include admin authorization checks and transaction logging.
Create separate standalone website for tournament match management with password protection (admin123)
Create separate standalone website for direct coin deposits to user wallets with password protection (admin123)

Create a new standalone website application specifically for tournament match management. This website must be completely separate from the main FF PAYALUGA application and should only be accessible via a distinct URL/deployment.
Implement password protection for the tournament match management website using the password 'admin123'. Users must enter this password before accessing any tournament match management features.

Include all tournament match management functionality in the new standalone website: view current matches, create new matches, update match status, record match results, manage match schedules, and search/filter matches.

Create a second standalone website application specifically for direct coin deposits to user wallets. This website must be completely separate from both the main FF PAYALUGA application and the tournament match management website, accessible via its own distinct URL/deployment.
Implement password protection for the direct coin deposit website using the password 'admin123'. Users must enter this password before accessing coin deposit functionality.

Include all direct coin deposit functionality in the new standalone website: user selection interface, coin amount input with validation, ability to manually add coins to any user's wallet, and transaction history viewing with search capabilities.

Apply the Free Fire gaming themed dark UI with gaming-style elements to both new standalone admin websites, ensuring visual consistency with the main application while maintaining their independent nature.
Update UI theme to match real Free Fire battle aesthetics with authentic game visuals and styling
Display owner/creator name 'NIRANJAN' prominently in the application interface
